## Part (a): Implementation

```dafny
function Canonicalize(x: Id): Id {
  Id(x.kind, x.value, if x.width >= PAD then x.width else PAD)
}
```

## Part (b): Lemma with `ensures` clauses

```dafny
lemma Spec(x: Id)
  requires Wellformed(x)
  // 1. Kind and value are never changed
  ensures Canonicalize(x).kind == x.kind
  ensures Canonicalize(x).value == x.value
  // 2. The canonical width is at least PAD (minimum 4 digits)
  ensures Canonicalize(x).width >= PAD
  // 3. If the input width is already >= PAD, it is returned unchanged (no-op)
  ensures x.width >= PAD ==> Canonicalize(x).width == x.width
  // 4. If the input width is below PAD, the canonical width is exactly PAD
  ensures x.width < PAD ==> Canonicalize(x).width == PAD
  // 5. The result is wellformed (canonical width can still hold the value)
  ensures Wellformed(Canonicalize(x))
  // 6. Canonicalizing an already-canonical id is a no-op
  ensures Canonicalize(Canonicalize(x)) == Canonicalize(x)
{ }
```

### Explanation of each clause

| Clause | Contract requirement covered |
|--------|------------------------------|
| `.kind == x.kind` | "The kind … must never change" |
| `.value == x.value` | "The numeric value must never change" |
| `.width >= PAD` | "minimum of 4 digits" |
| `x.width >= PAD ==> .width == x.width` | "An id already at 4 or more digits is returned unchanged" |
| `x.width < PAD ==> .width == PAD` | Left-zero-padded to exactly 4 when below minimum |
| `Wellformed(result)` | The output is itself wellformed (width can hold value) |
| `Canonicalize(Canonicalize(x)) == Canonicalize(x)` | "Canonicalizing an already-canonical id is a no-op" (idempotence) |
